MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER Plaintiffs Ninth Through Fifteenth Causes Of Action From Her First Through Eighth Causes Of Action And For Separate Trials
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Feb-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adalberto Corres, Esq. of VILLEGAS CARRERA, LLP, Tel. No. 415.989.8000, appearing for the plaintiff/moving party.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

Ruling - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted in part, denied in part. There shall be no discovery of the workers compensation file relating to the claim for head/neck injury, and the court orders the subpoena quashed as to that discovery. There is no personal injury claimed in the present case, and thus records pertaining to injury to a part of the body not implicated here is barred by Britt v. Superior Court, 20 Cal. 3d 844, 864 (1978). The court finds that the subject matter of the workers compensation claim #E1A7326 filed on or about June 11 (the stress claim) may well contain matter directly relevant to the present case. The court orders that defendant may issue a revised subpoena seeking information in that claim limited to sexual harassment, abuse or discrimination in plaintiff's workplace. Plaintiff's claim of emotional or psychological injury in the present case does not operate as a wholesale waiver of her privacy rights. See Britt at 864.
Sanctions request by plaintiff denied based on noncompliance with notice requirements of CCP 2023.040. Defendants' request for sanctions denied as the court finds plaintiff had substantial justification for making this motion. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HK)
